title: thebasics:thebasics-handbook-title-rp-chat
text: RP chat is the core of The BASICs. When enabled, normal chat becomes proximity-based roleplay text with configurable ranges, formatting, overhead bubbles, OOC behavior, and chatter sounds. Server config can turn the RP pipeline on or off, change ranges, require nicknames or character sheet fields, enable or disable shortcuts, and gate commands by privilege. Speech modes - /say [message], aliases /s and /normal: speak normally or set your default mode to normal. - /whisper [message], alias /w: whisper once or set whisper as your default mode. - /yell [message], alias /y: yell once or set yell as your default mode. Server config can tune each mode's range or disable the mode. Emotes and environment text - /me <emote>: proximity emote. - /it <message> or /do <message>: nearby environmental description. - /envhere <message>: place environment text where you are looking; if no surface is hit, it falls back above you. - /emotemode [on|off]: make ordinary proximity messages act like emotes unless another syntax says otherwise. Nicknames Nicknames are part of RP chat and predate character sheets. - /nick [name]: view or set your RP display name when enabled. - /clearnick: clear your nickname. - /nickcolor [color] and /clearnickcolor: manage nickname color when enabled. If character sheets are also enabled, nickname commands can update the configured nickname sheet field, so nicknames also appear in the Character Sheets page. Typing shortcuts Server config controls these shortcuts. Defaults may include *text for emote, !text for environment, !!text for placed environment text, //text for OOC, (text) for low/OOC, and ((text)) for global OOC. Visual and audio helpers - Overhead chat bubbles, typing indicators, and chatter sounds are config toggles. - /chatprefs opens color preferences; /chatprefs help lists commands. - /chatprefs preset <highcontrast|classic|default> can quickly improve readability.
